county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Price County formed 1879 from Chippewa and Lincoln counties. County seat is Phillips. Northern Wisconsin lumber county. German, Scandinavian, and Finnish settlement. Wisconsin Concrete Park (Fred Smith).
county_metadata.research_tips: Price County Register of Deeds in Phillips holds land records from 1879. Created from Chippewa and Lincoln counties. Northern Wisconsin lumber county. German, Scandinavian, and Finnish settlers followed the timber industry. The Price County Historical Society in Phillips maintains collections. WHS has microfilmed Price County records. Wisconsin Concrete Park (Fred Smith sculptures). Flambeau River State Forest. Lumber company employment records at WHS. FamilySearch has limited Price County records. BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries. Check Chippewa and Lincoln counties for pre-1879 records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Price County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Phillips; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Price County Deed Books -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.repository: Price County Register of Deeds, Phillips
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original deed books for Price County from 1879; grantor/grantee indexes; mortgages and liens; Register of Deeds office maintains land records. WHS has microfilm of historical deed books
source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1879; no major record losses

source_profiles.source_name: Price County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents (preemption, homestead, cash entry, military bounty) for Price County; Wisconsin is a public land state and GLO patents document original federal land transfers to private ownership

source_profiles.repository: Price County Probate Court, Phillips
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, inventories, guardianships, and estate settlements for Price County from 1879; Probate Court has jurisdiction over estate matters; WHS has microfilm of historical probate records

source_profiles.repository: Wisconsin Historical Society, Madison

source_profiles.repository: Wisconsin Historical Society, Madison
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Historic newspapers for Price County towns at WHS on microfilm and digital; legal notices, obituaries, marriage announcements, estate settlements, and community news; WHS has one of the nation's largest newspaper collections

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Racine County formed 1836 from Milwaukee County. County seat is Racine on Lake Michigan. One of Wisconsin's original counties. Major manufacturing center. 'Most Danish city in America.' Also German, Czech, and African American communities.
county_metadata.research_tips: Racine County Register of Deeds in Racine holds land records from 1836. One of Wisconsin's original counties on Lake Michigan. Major manufacturing center (J.I. Case, S.C. Johnson). Very strong Danish immigrant community -- Racine was called 'the most Danish city in America.' Also significant German, Czech (Bohemian), and African American communities. The Racine Heritage Museum has excellent collections. WHS has microfilmed Racine County records. Danish Brotherhood and Dania Society records. Racine city directories from the 1850s. Wind Point Lighthouse records. FamilySearch has indexed Racine County records. Catholic, Lutheran, and Danish church records essential.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Racine County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Racine; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1836; no major record losses

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Richland County formed 1842 from Iowa County. County seat is Richland Center. Southwestern Wisconsin driftless area county. Frank Lloyd Wright birthplace (1867). Norwegian, German, and Irish settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Richland County Register of Deeds in Richland Center holds land records from 1842. Southwestern Wisconsin driftless area county. Frank Lloyd Wright's birthplace in Richland Center (1867). Norwegian, German, and Irish immigrant settlement. The Richland County Historical Society in Richland Center maintains collections. WHS has microfilmed Richland County records. Taliesin (Wright's home/studio) is in neighboring Iowa/Sauk area. FamilySearch has some Richland County records. BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries. Church records (Norwegian Lutheran, Catholic, Methodist) supplement civil records. Lead mining also occurred in western Richland County.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Richland County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Richland Center; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1842; no major record losses

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Rock County formed 1836 from Milwaukee County. County seat is Janesville. One of Wisconsin's original counties in the southern tier. Major agricultural and manufacturing center. Beloit College (1846). Underground Railroad history.
county_metadata.research_tips: Rock County Register of Deeds in Janesville holds land records from 1836. One of Wisconsin's original counties in the southern tier. Major agricultural and manufacturing center (General Motors/Janesville Assembly). Beloit and Janesville had diverse industrial workforces. Yankee, Norwegian, Irish, and German settlement. The Rock County Historical Society in Janesville has excellent collections including the Helen Jeffris Wood Museum Center. Beloit College archives. WHS has microfilmed Rock County records. Tallman House (Abraham Lincoln visited). Janesville and Beloit city directories from the 1850s. FamilySearch has indexed Rock County records. Underground Railroad sites documented.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Rock County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Janesville; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1836; no major record losses

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Rusk County formed 1901 from Chippewa County (originally Gates County, renamed 1905). County seat is Ladysmith. Northern Wisconsin lumber county. Czech, German, and Scandinavian settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Rusk County Register of Deeds in Ladysmith holds land records from 1901. Originally named Gates County (renamed Rusk 1905). Created from Chippewa County -- check Chippewa County for pre-1901 records. Northern Wisconsin lumber county. Czech (Bohemian), German, and Scandinavian immigrants settled here. The Rusk County Historical Society in Ladysmith maintains collections. WHS has microfilmed Rusk County records. Flambeau River lumber drives. School district records supplement census data. FamilySearch has limited Rusk County records. BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Rusk County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Ladysmith; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Sauk County formed 1840 from Crawford County. County seat is Baraboo. South-central Wisconsin driftless area county. Ringling Brothers circus originated in Baraboo (1884). Circus World Museum. Devil's Lake State Park. Ho-Chunk Nation presence.
county_metadata.research_tips: Sauk County Register of Deeds in Baraboo holds land records from 1840. South-central Wisconsin county in the scenic Baraboo Range and driftless area. Circus heritage -- Ringling Brothers originated in Baraboo (1884); Circus World Museum has Ringling family and circus employee records. Devil's Lake State Park area. German, Irish, and English settlement. The Sauk County Historical Society in Baraboo has extensive collections. WHS has microfilmed Sauk County records. Al. Ringling Theatre. Ho-Chunk Nation presence. FamilySearch has indexed Sauk County records. BLM GLO patents. Church records (Catholic, Lutheran, Methodist) supplement civil records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Sauk County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Baraboo; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1840; no major record losses

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Sawyer County formed 1883 from Ashland and Chippewa counties. County seat is Hayward. Northern Wisconsin lake and forest county. Lac Courte Oreilles Band of Lake Superior Chippewa reservation. Lumber and tourism heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: Sawyer County Register of Deeds in Hayward holds land records from 1883. Created from Ashland and Chippewa counties. Northern Wisconsin lake and forest county. The Lac Courte Oreilles Band of Lake Superior Chippewa reservation is in Sawyer County -- tribal records at NARA Great Lakes Region. Lumber industry records at WHS. Tourism records (fishing, hunting resorts). The Sawyer County Historical Society in Hayward maintains collections. WHS has microfilmed Sawyer County records. Scandinavian and German settlement. Hayward is home to the National Fresh Water Fishing Hall of Fame. FamilySearch has limited Sawyer County records. BLM GLO patents.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Sawyer County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Hayward; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Shawano County formed 1853 from Oconto County. County seat is Shawano. Northeastern Wisconsin county. Stockbridge-Munsee Band of Mohicans reservation. Menominee Reservation borders to the north. German settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Shawano County Register of Deeds in Shawano holds land records from 1853. Northeastern Wisconsin county. The Stockbridge-Munsee Band of Mohicans reservation is in western Shawano County -- tribal records at NARA Great Lakes Region. Menominee Reservation borders to the north. German immigrants were the primary settler group. The Shawano County Historical Society in Shawano maintains collections. Heritage Park museum complex. WHS has microfilmed Shawano County records. Catholic and Lutheran church records (Diocese of Green Bay) are essential. FamilySearch has some Shawano County records. BLM GLO patents.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Shawano County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Shawano; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Sheboygan County formed 1836 from Brown County. County seat is Sheboygan on Lake Michigan. One of Wisconsin's original counties. Very strong German and Dutch immigrant communities. Kohler Company. 'The Wurst City.'
county_metadata.research_tips: Sheboygan County Register of Deeds in Sheboygan holds land records from 1836. One of Wisconsin's original counties on Lake Michigan. Very strong German and Dutch immigrant communities -- Sheboygan was known as 'the wurst city' for its German sausage-making tradition. Also significant Hmong community (more recent). The Sheboygan County Historical Society and Research Center in Sheboygan Falls has excellent collections. WHS has microfilmed Sheboygan County records. Dutch immigrant settlement in Cedar Grove and Oostburg -- Holland Christian Reformed Church records essential. Kohler Company records document workers. Sheboygan city directories from the 1860s. FamilySearch has indexed many Sheboygan County records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Sheboygan County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Sheboygan; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Sheboygan County Deed Books -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.repository: Sheboygan County Register of Deeds, Sheboygan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original deed books for Sheboygan County from 1836; grantor/grantee indexes; mortgages and liens; Register of Deeds office maintains land records. WHS has microfilm of historical deed books
source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1836; no major record losses

source_profiles.source_name: Sheboygan County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents (preemption, homestead, cash entry, military bounty) for Sheboygan County; Wisconsin is a public land state and GLO patents document original federal land transfers to private ownership

source_profiles.source_name: Sheboygan County Probate Court Records
source_profiles.repository: Sheboygan County Probate Court, Sheboygan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, inventories, guardianships, and estate settlements for Sheboygan County from 1836; Probate Court has jurisdiction over estate matters; WHS has microfilm of historical probate records

source_profiles.source_name: Sheboygan County Marriage Records -- County Clerk / WHS / FamilySearch
source_profiles.repository: Sheboygan County Clerk, Sheboygan / Wisconsin Historical Society, Madison
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage records for Sheboygan County from 1836; County Clerk issued marriage licenses in Wisconsin; WHS holds some county marriage records on microfilm; FamilySearch has indexed Wisconsin marriages; state vital records registration began 1907 (sporadic earlier compliance)

source_profiles.source_name: Sheboygan County Federal and State Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry / WHS
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1840-1950 for Sheboygan County; Wisconsin state census 1855, 1865, 1875, 1885, 1895, 1905 at WHS (unusually detailed -- include place of birth, occupation, citizenship status); territorial census 1836 and 1842 may include Sheboygan County entries

source_profiles.source_name: Sheboygan County German Church Records -- Catholic / Lutheran Parishes
source_profiles.repository: Local parishes / Catholic Archdiocese of Milwaukee or Diocese archives / Wisconsin Historical Society, Madison
source_profiles.coverage_notes: German Catholic and Lutheran parish registers (baptisms, marriages, burials) for Sheboygan County; many parish registers kept in German until the 1900s; Catholic records through archdiocese/diocese archives; Lutheran records (Missouri Synod, Wisconsin Synod) through church archives; WHS has transcribed some German church records; essential for German immigrant family research

source_profiles.source_name: Sheboygan County Cemetery Records -- Find A Grave / WI GenWeb
source_profiles.repository: Find A Grave / Wisconsin GenWeb / Sheboygan County Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Sheboygan County including church cemeteries, municipal cemeteries, and pioneer family burial grounds; Find A Grave has growing coverage; Wisconsin GenWeb and local historical societies have published transcriptions; DAR chapters have documented many Wisconsin cemeteries

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: St. Croix County formed 1840 from Crawford County. County seat is Hudson on the St. Croix River opposite Minnesota. Western Wisconsin lumber and farming county. Scandinavian, German, and Irish settlement. Parent county for Pierce and Polk.
county_metadata.research_tips: St. Croix County Register of Deeds in Hudson holds land records from 1840. Western Wisconsin border county on the St. Croix River across from Minnesota. Early lumber industry -- St. Croix River was a major log drive route. Scandinavian (Norwegian, Swedish), German, and Irish settlement. The St. Croix County Historical Society in Hudson maintains collections. Octagon House Museum in Hudson. WHS has microfilmed St. Croix County records. Willow River State Park. Hudson city records supplement county records. FamilySearch has some St. Croix County records. BLM GLO patents. St. Croix County is a parent county for Pierce and Polk.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for St. Croix County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Hudson; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.source_name: St. Croix County Historic Newspapers -- WHS
source_profiles.repository: Wisconsin Historical Society, Madison
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Historic newspapers for St. Croix County towns at WHS on microfilm and digital; legal notices, obituaries, marriage announcements, estate settlements, and community news; WHS has one of the nation's largest newspaper collections
